authoritative_sources.title: Chapter 69O Internet and Mobile Gaming Systems
authoritative_sources.publisher: New Jersey Division of Gaming Enforcement
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official New Jersey gaming regulation governing patron account registration, identity verification, account statements, deposits, withdrawals, and responsible-gaming controls for internet and mobile gaming systems.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Player-account intake, account-status review, withdrawal handling, and player communication grounding
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains account-status, wallet, withdrawal, and communication scenarios so the lane asks for player id, property, jurisdiction, and account-ledger evidence before resolving or escalating.

authoritative_sources.title: Chapter 69N Sports Wagering
authoritative_sources.publisher: New Jersey Division of Gaming Enforcement
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official New Jersey sports-wagering regulation defining house rules, patron complaint points of contact, prohibited wagers, suspicious-activity escalation, and other operational controls that directly shape player-service responses.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Sportsbook request classification, house-rule handling, and patron-complaint routing
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps settled-wager or prohibited-wager issues out of generic service handling and forces sportsbook-settlement or compliance handoff when the case requires adjudication.

authoritative_sources.title: Chapter 69G Self-Exclusion and Exclusion
authoritative_sources.publisher: New Jersey Division of Gaming Enforcement
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official New Jersey self-exclusion and exclusion regulation used to define self-exclusion enrollment, confidentiality, operator obligations, and the prohibition on reactivating excluded players through ordinary service handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Responsible-gaming restriction review, exclusion handling, and marketing or account reactivation boundary control
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from removing exclusions, overriding cooling-off controls, or treating exclusion work as a discretionary customer-service decision.

authoritative_sources.title: Regulation 7A Patron Disputes
authoritative_sources.publisher: Nevada Gaming Commission and Nevada Gaming Control Board
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Nevada patron-dispute regulation defining when disputes must be documented, what records must be preserved, and how unresolved patron disputes must be escalated beyond frontline service.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Patron dispute intake, evidence preservation, and regulator-safe escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Shapes the escalation packet requirements and keeps unresolved patron disputes from being closed as ordinary service tickets.

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Internal Control Standards for Interactive Gaming, Version 9
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Nevada internal-control standard defining player-registration records, account restrictions, self-exclusion checks, system logging, and cashless or interactive-gaming evidence controls relevant to player-account servicing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: System-of-record validation, control checks, and player-account evidence review
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Provides the control vocabulary for player-registration, restrictions, transaction logs, and account access checks used in evidence-traceability and dependency handling.

authoritative_sources.title: Frequently Asked Questions: Casino Recordkeeping, Reporting, and Compliance Program Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FinCEN guidance for casinos and card clubs that defines required records, suspicious-transaction reporting posture, and AML program expectations when player-services work touches cash, accounts, and suspicious activity.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cash-sensitive request handling, suspicious-activity controls, and refusal to disclose AML posture
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from disclosing suspicious-activity posture or treating cash or account anomalies as routine service matters when AML controls are implicated.

authoritative_sources.title: Recognizing Suspicious Activity: Red Flags for Casinos and Card Clubs
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FinCEN red-flag guidance describing suspicious player, cash, and chip activity patterns that require escalation instead of ordinary player-service resolution.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Suspicious-player, chip, and cash pattern triage
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Adds casino-specific red flags such as structured chip or cash behavior, third-party activity, and unusual funding patterns to the refusal and escalation model.

authoritative_sources.title: Responsible Gaming Code of Conduct
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AGA's Responsible Gaming Code is a recognized public gaming-industry framework for employee training, player-facing information, and non-exploitative communication expectations that shape player-services workflow.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Player communication, responsible-gaming posture, and support-team conduct
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains player-facing communication so the lane avoids pressure tactics, unsupported promises, or language that undermines self-exclusion and responsible-gaming controls.

workflow_stages.name: Service intake and player-context grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm player, property, jurisdiction, request channel, and core identifiers such as player-card number, wagering-account id, wager id, withdrawal id, ticket number, or complaint reference before acting.

workflow_stages.name: Record-of-truth and evidence retrieval
workflow_stages.description: Review player-account, loyalty, wagering, withdrawal, cage, ticket, self-exclusion, complaint, and communication records before stating status, next steps, or restrictions.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Supported request resolution and bounded communication
workflow_stages.description: Respond using only documented account, wallet, or promotion status, house-rule language, and responsible-gaming-safe next steps without promising reversals, exemptions, or internal investigative outcomes.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Patron dispute, repeat-failure, and complaint escalation
workflow_stages.description: Package unresolved patron disputes, repeat service failures, or house-rule conflicts with preserved evidence and the correct next owner instead of retrying unsupported fixes.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Cash, suspicious-activity, and exclusion boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when cash, chip, wallet, third-party funding, self-exclusion, or suspicious-activity facts materially change the safe answer and do not disclose internal control or reporting posture.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: player-account profiles, loyalty records, player-card records, and communication logs
wager ids, sportsbook or igaming transaction histories, wallet ledgers, withdrawal records, and bonus or promotion records
TITO tickets, kiosk receipts, cage slips, redemption logs, and cash-related exception notes
self-exclusion, cooling-off, marketing-suppression, KYC, geolocation, and account-restriction records
patron complaint forms, patron dispute logs, case notes, and escalation packets
AML, suspicious-activity, surveillance, and approval-history markers needed to identify refusal or routing boundaries
systems_of_record: cage, kiosk, or cash-desk tracking system
surveillance, fraud, and AML monitoring systems

allowed: Classify player-service work as in-scope, blocked, handoff-ready, or unresolved based on documented player-account, wager, cash, dispute, and exclusion artifacts.
Prepare evidence-backed resolution notes, player communication summaries, and escalation packets without claiming wagering, AML, surveillance, or regulatory final authority.
Request or validate the minimum tenant facts, systems, and source records needed to continue safely.
escalate: Conflicting public guidance, jurisdiction rules, house rules, or tenant policy that materially change what may be communicated, redeemed, reversed, or withdrawn.
Requests requiring sportsbook settlement, cage signoff, responsible-gaming authority, compliance approval, AML review, surveillance review, or regulator-facing handling.
Repeated player-service failures where blocker history shows the issue is not being resolved in the current lane.
refuse: Remove self-exclusion or responsible-gaming restrictions, or promise reinstatement, marketing reactivation, or account reopening without the authorized path.
Approve cash, credit, bonus, comp, refund, withdrawal, or third-party transfer exceptions without documented authority.
Treat casinos, gaming, and wagering player services as generic support that ignores player-account, wager, cage, house-rule, dispute, or exclusion evidence.

domain_failure_modes: player-service work proceeds without property, jurisdiction, or player-account identifiers that materially change the answer
self-excluded or restricted players are treated as ordinary service cases and receive unsupported reinstatement or marketing guidance
settled-wager or house-rule disputes are answered as customer-service questions instead of routed to settlement, compliance, or dispute workflows
cash, chip, wallet, or withdrawal anomalies are treated as normal service issues despite AML or suspicious-activity indicators
generic support language replaces gaming-specific records such as player-card status, wager ids, TITO tickets, cage slips, self-exclusion records, and house rules
cross-property or cross-system contradictions in loyalty, wallet, cage, or player-account records are ignored instead of escalated as evidence conflicts

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane
Scenario proving escalation when industry-specific authority or safety facts are missing
Scenario proving the lane refuses or escalates when the industry name cannot be treated as cosmetic
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific time limits and submission paths for patron disputes, ticket redemption, and self-exclusion requests vary across states and properties.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Could change the exact escalation owner, form, or response timeline.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_policy: Treat jurisdiction and property as required tenant facts; escalate when the relevant jurisdictional source or tenant routing path is missing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Operators implement different wallet providers, cross-property loyalty structures, and promotion terms for bonus or comp balances.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Could change which ledger is authoritative and whether a bonus or withdrawal state is reversible.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_policy: Require tenant system-of-record mapping and promotion terms before resolving wallet, bonus, or comp disputes.

notes: The research set intentionally combines state gaming regulations, gaming-control standards, federal casino AML guidance, and recognized responsible-gaming guidance so workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling are all covered.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for this validated pack because the variable areas are turned into explicit tenant-fact and jurisdiction-retrieval prerequisites.
